segments.text: This is the Backfocus Manager, in the Equipment Manager. It builds your imaging train, draws it to scale, and works out the exact spacers to hit your sensor's backfocus.
Start on the left. Add each part of your train from the vendor inventory — adapters, spacers, filter drawers, and your camera. AstroIndexer knows every part's optical length.
segments.caption: Build from the vendor inventory

segments.text: Your train is drawn to scale, from the reference optic down to the sensor plane, so the spacing is obvious at a glance.
segments.caption: Drawn to scale, optic to sensor

segments.text: The backfocus summary shows your target and tolerance. The under, within, over band tells you at a glance whether you're in spec.
segments.caption: Under · within · over

segments.text: Filter-glass compensation is folded into the maths, and the details panel resolves the spacer combination you need.
segments.caption: Filter-glass + spacer maths

segments.text: When it's dialled in, export the checklist and take it to the scope.
segments.caption: Export the checklist

segments.text: That's the Backfocus Manager — dial in your train before you head out.
